course: Power Punching Blueprint 2.0 / Manual of Modern Boxing Biomechanics
author: Alex Wiant DC
status: rough draft — covers through lead hook (uppercut, wrapping, bag work still 1.0-only)
notes: 2.0 is a decade-later rewrite of PPB 1.0. New framing: Kinetic Integrated Mechanics (KIMs) system, kinetic tether, athletic center, Myers myofascial chains named explicitly, natural vs dynamic loading, triple threat stance, diaphragm feedforward. 1.0 chunks (01-15) kept as-is; they still solely cover uppercut, hand wrapping and bag work.

chunks.topic: Premise, boxing trails other sports in biomechanics, born-with-power myth, 2.0 roadmap
Stance as athletic ready position, ground reaction force, why linear pivoting fails
SSC, elastic muscle energy, stretch-and-contract is not conscious, reflexive bracing
Load, explode, acceleration, impact/release, follow through, reset as chained phases
COM vs athletic center (hara/dantian), leading movement from the athletic center
Many chains not one, Thomas Myers, kinetic tether, tetherball analogy
Posterior chain structures + hip hinge primer, glutes vs hamstrings
Spiral line: obliques, serratus anterior, rotational force transfer
Front functional line: abs, pec major, lats context
Arm lines: pecs, biceps, triceps, wrist flexors; every punch arcs
Lateral line + quads power the jab; bladed-stance lateral-to-forward translation
Three core layers, antenna/guy wires, resting tension, IAP, diaphragm feedforward
Hinge deep dive, bamboo spine, head-past-knee nuance, wall drill, hinge exercises
Loading through movement — slips/bobs/weaves as hidden load phases
Module 1: stance width, gravity, bouncing-ball plyometric weight shifting, triple threat stance, sitting down clarified
Module 2: natural vs dynamic loading, one-inch dip, bounce exercises 1-4
Module 3: opening/closing hips (Peter Ralston), explosive start, closed-position stability
Module 4: spaghetti arms, shot shaping, high five drills
Module 5: last-three-knuckle landing, lat + wrist flexor connections
Module 6: fist grab, vectors, golden spiral, shearing force, protraction, body lean, amplitude, reset, troubleshooting
Adaptability, GRF-leg requirement, phase 2/3 overlap
Jab: lateral line + quads, hip flick, stepping, jab to the body
Rear hand: entries (after jab, lead right, slip/pull counter), hips-torso-arm, elliptical reset
Lead hook: flat-foot glute drive, scapular retraction/snap, bicep arc-tightening, hip counter-rotation reset
